page: Amazon Web Services c8g.medium is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Utilizing the ARM64 architecture, this instance features 1 dedicated physical core and 1 vCPU running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware-accelerated AES cryptography support. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows a split performance profile. Single-core processing is a relative strength, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance landing in the average tier, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression both achieving strong ratings. However, due to its single-core limitation, multi-core benchmarks such as stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores fall into the poor category. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block write speeds at 11.61 GB/sec and read-only small-block cached bandwidth at 115.94 GB/sec, both ranking in the lower percentiles. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput similarly register in the lower performance tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8g.medium offers a low-entry-point hardware profile for ARM64 development and testing. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable single-threaded execution without the CPU credit exhaustion risks of burstable instances. This makes it suitable for lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic utility scripting, ARM64 software compilation testing, and low-traffic web serving or microservices that do not require high memory density or multi-threaded parallel processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 server instance powered by a single-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 1 dedicated vCPU, 2.0 GB of memory, and a 1 Gbps baseline network interface, with no local storage or GPU resources. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers average single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression and decompression speeds, though its single-core design limits multi-core and memory-intensive benchmarks to lower tiers. The c8g.medium provides a cost-efficient entry point for ARM64 development, lightweight single-threaded microservices, and basic utility workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8g.medium is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance with 1 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 core, 2.0 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A single-core ARM64 compute instance offering dedicated processor execution and strong single-threaded performance for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor
Equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory and 36 MB of L3 cache
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression benchmarks
Features a 1 Gbps baseline network speed on the Nitro hypervisor
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for ARM64 development, testing, and lightweight single-threaded tasks
